DISCOVERY 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATION. PLAINTIFF ROBERT MENDALA'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S DOCUMENT PRODUCTION REQUEST TO DEFENDANT TIEMPO INTERIORS, SET TWO IS GRANTED IN PART. TAX RETURNS AND RELATED DOCUMENTS KEPT BY DEFENDANT'S ACCOUNTANT ARE DEEMED TO BE WITHIN ITS CUSTODY AND CONTROL. THE DOCUMENTS ARE RELEVANT TO THE TAMENY CLAIMS. DEFENDANT SHALL PRODUCE ITS TAX RETURNS FOR 2004, 2005 AND 2006. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO DISTINGUISH SCHNABEL V. SUP. CT. (1993) 5 CAL 4TH 704. THE RECORDS AND DATA UPON WHICH TAX RETURNS ARE BASED ARE SUBJECT TO DISCOVERY. SEE WEIL & BROWN ET AL. CAL PRAC. GUIDE:CIV PRO. BEFORE TRIAL (TRG 2011) 8:122. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ALL DOCUMENTS "CONCERNING" TAX RETURNS IS OVERBROAD. DEFENDANT MUST PRODUCE AN AMENDED WRITTEN RESPONSE TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION: (1) ALL BANK STATEMENTS OF TIEMPO INTERIORS, INC. SUFFICIENT TO SHOW ALL DEPOSITS MADE IN 2004, 2005 AND 2006; (2) ANY OTHER DOCUMENTS RECORDING CASH RECEIPTS OF TIEMPO INTERIORS, INC. NOT DEPOSITED INTO A BANK ACCOUNT IN 2004, 2005, AND 2006. IF PLAINTIFF BELIEVES THESE DOCUMENTS ARE INSUFFICIENT TO ATTEMPT TO PROVE HIS CLAIMS, COUNSEL MAY APPEAR AT THE HEARING TO SEEK TO MODIFY THIS ORDER. ALL DOCUMENTS MUST BE PRODUCED BY MARCH 3, 2013. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED TO PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $840. SUCH SANCTIONS SHALL BE PAID ON OR BEFORE MARCH 3, 2013. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. PROTEM JUDGE: TOM COHEN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S Document Production Req. To Deft Tiempo Interiors, Set Two
Off Calendar Feb-21-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: KEVIN FLAUTT, ESQ., 415.788.7600, FOR PLAINTIFF ROBERT MENDALA.
ELTON BLUM, ESQ., 415.788.1666, FOR DEFENDANT IRENE CHEUNG.

RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATION. PLAINTIFF ROBERT MENDALA'S MOTION To Compel Further Responses To PLAINTIFF'S Document Production ReqUEST To DefENDANt Tiempo Interiors, Set Two IS GRANTED in part. Tax returns and related documents kept by Defendant's accountant are deemed to be within its custody and control. The documents are relevant to the Tameny claims. Defendant shall produce its tax returns for 2004, 2005 and 2006. Defendant has failed to distinguish Schnabel v. Sup. Ct. (1993) 5 Cal 4th 704. The records and data upon which tax returns are based are subject to discovery. See Weil & Brown et al. CAL PRAC. GUIDE:CIV PRO. BEFORE TRIAL (TRG 2011) 8:122. Plaintiff's request for all documents "concerning" tax returns is overbroad. Defendant must produce AN AMENDED WRITTEN RESPONSE TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION: (1) all bank statements of Tiempo Interiors, Inc. sufficient to show all deposits made in 2004, 2005 and 2006; (2) any other documents recording cash receipts of Tiempo Interiors, Inc. not deposited into a bank account in 2004, 2005, and 2006. If Plaintiff believes these documents are insufficient to attempt to prove his claims, counsel may appear at the hearing to seek to modify this order. All documents must be produced by March 3, 2013. Sanctions are awarded to Plaintiff in the amount of $840. SUCH SANCTIONS SHALL BE PAID ON OR BEFORE MARCH 3, 2013. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Protem Judge: Tom Cohen, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, not reported.
